2481	/-! ### The Nitsure §1 inverse construction: chart loci, chart matrices, chart morphisms
2482	
2483	For a rank-`d` quotient `x = ⟨F, q⟩` on `T` and a size-`d` subset `I ⊆ Fin r`, the
2484	*chart composite* is `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (the `I`-indexed coordinate inclusion
2485	followed by the quotient map) and the *chart locus* `T_I ⊆ T` is the largest open on
2486	which it restricts to an isomorphism. The loci are open by construction (a supremum of
2487	opens), cover `T` (Nakayama at each point), and over `T_I` the quotient is presented by
2488	a `d × r` matrix of sections whose `I`-minor is the identity — its complementary
2489	entries determine a ring map `R^I ⟶ Γ(T_I, O)` and hence a morphism `T_I ⟶ U^I` by the
2490	Γ–Spec adjunction. These glue to the inverse `grPointOfRankQuotient` of the universal
2491	property. -/

2538	/-- **Right-invertible matrices over a field have an invertible column minor**: a
2539	`d × r` matrix with a right inverse has an invertible `d × d` submatrix on the columns

2541	`chartComposite`). Pure linear algebra: the columns span `K^d`, a spanning set contains
2542	a basis, and a square matrix with independent columns is invertible. Project-local
2543	helper for the Nakayama covering step. -/
2544	private lemma exists_isUnit_submatrix {K : Type} [Field K] {d r : ℕ}
2545	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) K) (G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) K) (hMG : M * G = 1) :
2546	    ∃ (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d),
2547	      IsUnit (M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := by
2548	  classical
2549	  -- the columns of `M` span `K^d` (a right inverse makes `mulVec` surjective)
2550	  have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range M.col) = ⊤ := by
2551	    rw [← Matrix.range_mulVecLin, LinearMap.range_eq_top]
2552	    intro v
2553	    refine ⟨G.mulVec v, ?_⟩
2554	    show M.mulVec (G.mulVec v) = v
2555	    rw [Matrix.mulVec_mulVec, hMG, Matrix.one_mulVec]
2556	  -- extract a linearly independent spanning subset `b` of the column set
2557	  obtain ⟨b, hbsub, hbspan, hbind⟩ := exists_linearIndependent K (Set.range M.col)
2558	  rw [hspan] at hbspan
2559	  haveI : Fintype b := ((Set.finite_range M.col).subset hbsub).fintype
2560	  -- `b` is a basis, so it has exactly `d` elements
2561	  let B : Module.Basis b K (Fin d → K) :=
2562	    Module.Basis.mk hbind (by rw [Subtype.range_coe, hbspan])
2563	  have hcard : Fintype.card b = d := by
2564	    have h1 := Module.finrank_eq_card_basis B
2565	    rw [Module.finrank_pi K, Fintype.card_fin] at h1
2566	    exact h1.symm
2567	  -- choose a column index for each element of `b`
2568	  have hchoice : ∀ v : b, ∃ j : Fin r, M.col j = (v : Fin d → K) := fun v => hbsub v.2
2569	  choose φ hφ using hchoice
2570	  have hφinj : Function.Injective φ := fun u v huv => by
2571	    apply Subtype.ext
2572	    rw [← hφ u, ← hφ v, huv]
2573	  have hIcard : (Finset.univ.image φ).card = d := by
2574	    rw [Finset.card_image_of_injective _ hφinj, Finset.card_univ, hcard]
2575	  refine ⟨Finset.univ.image φ, hIcard, ?_⟩
2576	  -- each enumerated column lies in `b`
2577	  have hcol : ∀ j : Fin d,
2578	      M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)) ∈ b := by
2579	    intro j
2580	    obtain ⟨v, -, hv⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2581	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j).2
2582	    rw [← hv, hφ v]
2583	    exact v.2
2584	  set c : Fin d → b :=
2585	    fun j => ⟨M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)), hcol j⟩
2586	    with hcdef
2587	  have hcinj : Function.Injective c := by
2588	    intro j j' hjj
2589	    have hcols : M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r))
2590	        = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) :=
2591	      congrArg Subtype.val hjj
2592	    obtain ⟨v, -, hv⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2593	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j).2
2594	    obtain ⟨v', -, hv'⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2595	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j').2
2596	    have hvv' : v = v' := by
2597	      apply Subtype.ext
2598	      calc (v : Fin d → K)

2600	        _ = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)) := by rw [hv]
2601	        _ = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) := hcols
2602	        _ = M.col (φ v') := by rw [hv']

2605	        = (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) := by
2606	      rw [← hv, ← hv', hvv']

4371	/-- **The local-to-global inverse of the universal property** (Nitsure §1): a rank-`d`
4372	locally free quotient `q : O_T^r ↠ F` determines a morphism `T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` — the gluing,
4373	over the open cover `{T_I}` of the chart loci (`chartLocus_isOpenCover`), of the
4374	composites `T_I ⟶ U^I ⟶ Gr(d,r)` of the chart morphisms (`chartMorphism`) with the
4375	glue-data immersions; the overlap compatibility is `chartMorphism_glue_compat`. -/
4376	noncomputable def grPointOfRankQuotient {T : Scheme.{0}} (d r : ℕ)
4377	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : T ⟶ scheme d r :=
4378	  (T.openCoverOfIsOpenCover _ (chartLocus_isOpenCover d r x)).glueMorphisms
4379	    (fun I => chartMorphism d r x I.1 I.2 ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I)
4380	    (fun I J => chartMorphism_glue_compat d r x I J)
4381	
4382	/-- The inverse construction is constant on equivalence classes of quotients: an
4383	isomorphism of targets commuting with the quotient maps induces the same chart loci
4384	(`chartLocus_rel`), the same presenting matrices (`chartMatrixHom` is unchanged since
4385	`q_y ≫ (c_y)⁻¹ = q_x ≫ f ≫ f⁻¹ ≫ (c_x)⁻¹ = q_x ≫ (c_x)⁻¹` after `chartComposite_rel`),
4386	hence the same chart morphisms and the same glued morphism (compare both gluings over
4387	the common cover via `Scheme.OpenCover.hom_ext` + `ι_glueMorphisms`). The remaining
4388	content is the transport of `chartMatrix` along the locus equality. -/
4389	lemma grPointOfRankQuotient_rel {T : Scheme.{0}} (d r : ℕ)
4390	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
4391	    grPointOfRankQuotient d r x = grPointOfRankQuotient d r y := by
4392	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
4393	  refine (T.openCoverOfIsOpenCover _ (chartLocus_isOpenCover d r x)).hom_ext _ _ (fun I => ?_)
4394	  have hL : chartLocus x I.1 I.2 = chartLocus y I.1 I.2 := chartLocus_rel ⟨f, hf⟩ I.1 I.2
4395	  -- the defining property of the two glued morphisms on their own chart loci
4396	  have hx : (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r x
4397	      = chartMorphism d r x I.1 I.2 ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by
4398	    rw [grPointOfRankQuotient]
4399	    exact Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms _ _ _ I
4400	  have hy : (chartLocus y I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r y
4401	      = chartMorphism d r y I.1 I.2 ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by
4402	    rw [grPointOfRankQuotient]
4403	    -- the cover must be given explicitly: the index `I` carries the `x`-cover's `I₀`,
4404	    -- which would otherwise steer unification to the wrong cover
4405	    exact Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms
4406	      (T.openCoverOfIsOpenCover _ (chartLocus_isOpenCover d r y)) _ _ I
4407	  -- the cover map of the `x`-cover factors through the (equal) `y`-locus
4408	  have hι : (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι
4409	      = T.homOfLE hL.le ≫ (chartLocus y I.1 I.2).ι := (T.homOfLE_ι hL.le).symm
4410	  change (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r x
4411	    = (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r y
4412	  rw [hx, chartMorphism_rel d r f hf I.1 I.2 hL.le, hι, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, hy]
4413	  rfl

4415	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
4416	the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
4417	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
4418	
4419	The forward map sends `ψ : T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` to the pullback `ψ^*(U, u)` of the tautological
4420	pair (`tautologicalRankQuotient`); naturality (`homEquiv_comp`) is the already-proven
4421	pseudofunctoriality `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at the tautological point. The
4422	inverse is the chart-by-chart construction `grPointOfRankQuotient`; the two inverse laws
4423	are the remaining content (they consume the chart restriction isomorphisms
4424	`universalQuotient_restrictionIso` and the glued-scheme universal property). -/
4425	noncomputable def represents (d r : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
4426	    (functor d r).RepresentableBy (scheme d r) where
4427	  homEquiv {T} :=
4428	    { toFun := fun ψ => Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback ψ (tautologicalRankQuotient d r))
4429	      invFun := Quotient.lift (grPointOfRankQuotient d r)
4430	        (fun _ _ h => grPointOfRankQuotient_rel d r h)
4431	      left_inv := fun ψ => by
4432	        -- `grPointOfRankQuotient (ψ^* (U, u)) = ψ`: chart-locally, the pulled-back
4433	        -- matrix of sections is the `ψ`-image of the universal one, so the glued
4434	        -- morphism is `ψ` by uniqueness in the glueMorphisms universal property.
4435	        sorry
4436	      right_inv := fun q => by
4437	        -- `(grPointOfRankQuotient x)^* (U, u) ~ x`: the chart restriction isomorphisms
4438	        -- identify the pullback of the universal pair with `x` chart by chart; the
4439	        -- identifications agree on overlaps, hence glue (sheaf-condition of `Hom`).
4440	        sorry }
4441	  homEquiv_comp {T T'} f g := by
4442	    -- pseudofunctoriality of `rqPullback` at the tautological point — this is
4443	    -- `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at `⟦(U, u)⟧`
4444	    have h := congrArg (fun m => m (Quotient.mk _ (tautologicalRankQuotient d r)))
4445	      ((functor d r).map_comp g.op f.op)
4446	    exact h
4447	
4448	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

2232	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
2233	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
2234	  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
2235	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))

2237	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
2238	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
2239	    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)

2243	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
2244	
2245	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
2246	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
2247	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
2248	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
2249	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
2250	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
2251	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
2252	
2253	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
2254	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
2255	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
2256	unchanged. -/
2257	
2258	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
2259	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
2260	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/

298	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
299	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
300	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
301	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
302	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
303	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
304	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
305	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
306	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
307	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
308	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
309	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
310	
311	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
312	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
313	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/
314	private lemma chartQuotientMap_ιFree (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
315	    (k : Fin d) :
316	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
317	        ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI k : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
318	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
319	  set A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) with hA
320	  set S := AlgebraicGeometry.Spec A with hS
321	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
322	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
323	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)) ≫
324	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct
325	            (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
326	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
327	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
328	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
329	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
330	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
331	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
332	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
333	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
334	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
335	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
336	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
337	  have lift_eq :
338	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
339	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
340	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
341	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
342	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
343	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
344	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
345	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
346	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
347	    by_cases hpk : p = k
348	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
349	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
350	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
351	  rfl
352	
353	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
354	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
355	of sheaves of modules. -/
356	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
357	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
358	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
359	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
360	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
361	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
362	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
363	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
364	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
365	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
366	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
367	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
368	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
369	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi
370	
371	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

939	    chartQuotientMap d r I hI
940	      = matrixEndRect ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
941	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso
942	            (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))).inv)) := rfl

944	/-! ### Entry extraction and the matrix presentation of free-sheaf morphisms
945	
946	(The block below was moved up from the chart-transport section so that the chart-loci
947	covering proof `chartLocus_isOpenCover` — which precedes that section — can use it.) -/
948	
949	/-- The `p`-th **projection of the free sheaf** `O_X^d ⟶ O_X`, through the
950	biproduct/coproduct comparison. Companion of `SheafOfModules.ιFree`; project-local. -/
951	noncomputable def projFree {X : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (p : Fin d) :
952	    SheafOfModules.free (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
953	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
954	    biproduct.π (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) p
955	
956	/-- The global section of `O_X` carried by an endomorphism of the unit sheaf of modules:
957	the value of the endomorphism at the unit section `1` over `⊤`. This inverts `scalarEnd`
958	(`unitEndSection_scalarEnd`); project-local. -/
959	noncomputable def unitEndSection {X : Scheme.{0}}
960	    (e : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
961	    Γ(X, ⊤) :=
962	  e.val.app (Opposite.op ⊤) (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
963	
964	/-- `unitEndSection` inverts `scalarEnd`: extracting the section of the scalar
965	endomorphism of `a` returns `a`. Project-local. -/
966	lemma unitEndSection_scalarEnd {X : Scheme.{0}} (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
967	    unitEndSection (scalarEnd a) = a := by
968	  rw [unitEndSection, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
969	  -- the restriction along `⊤ ≤ ⊤` is the identity (`homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by proof
970	  -- irrelevance, definitionally)
971	  change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (𝟙 (Opposite.op (⊤ : X.Opens))) a) = a
972	  rw [X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_id]
973	  rfl
974	
975	/-- Every endomorphism of the unit sheaf is the scalar endomorphism of its global
976	section: `scalarEnd (unitEndSection e) = e`. Converse of `unitEndSection_scalarEnd`;
977	together they make `unitEndSection` a bijection. Project-local. -/
978	lemma scalarEnd_unitEndSection {X : Scheme.{0}}
979	    (e : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
980	    scalarEnd (unitEndSection e) = e := by
981	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
982	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
983	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
984	  -- LHS at `Y` is the restriction of `e`'s value at `⊤`; RHS is `e`'s value at `Y`,
985	  -- equal by naturality of `e.val` at `Y ≤ ⊤` (and `1|_Y = 1`).
986	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op
987	      (e.val.app (Opposite.op ⊤) (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)))
988	    = e.val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
989	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply e.val
990	    (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
991	  refine hnat.symm.trans (congrArg (fun z => e.val.app Y z) ?_)
992	  exact PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one X.ringCatSheaf.obj
993	    (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op
994	
995	/-- Composing the `k`-th free injection with the `p`-th free projection is the identity
996	when `k = p` and zero otherwise. Project-local helper for entry extraction. -/
997	lemma ιFree_projFree {X : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (k p : Fin d) :
998	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := X.ringCatSheaf) k ≫ projFree p
999	      = if k = p then 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) else 0 := by
1000	  rw [projFree, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
1001	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
1002	  rw [biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_π]
1003	  by_cases h : k = p
1004	  · subst h; rw [dif_pos rfl, if_pos rfl]; rfl
1005	  · rw [dif_neg h, if_neg h]
1006	
1007	/-- **Entry extraction for `matrixEndRect`**: the `(p, j)` unit-sheaf component of the
1008	rectangular matrix morphism is the scalar endomorphism of the `(p, j)` entry.
1009	Project-local. -/
1010	lemma ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1011	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (j : Fin r) (p : Fin d) :
1012	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p
1013	      = scalarEnd (M p j) := by
1014	  rw [← Category.assoc, ιFree_matrixEndRect, Preadditive.sum_comp]
1015	  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single p (fun k _ hk => ?_)
1016	    (fun hp => absurd (Finset.mem_univ p) hp)).trans ?_
1017	  · rw [Category.assoc, ιFree_projFree, if_neg hk, Limits.comp_zero]
1018	  · rw [Category.assoc, ιFree_projFree, if_pos rfl, Category.comp_id]
1019

1021	/-- **Matrix presentation of a morphism of free sheaves**: any `φ : O_S^r ⟶ O_S^d` is
1022	`matrixEndRect` of its matrix of unit-component sections. Project-local — the
1023	extensionality that lets the pullback-naturality of `matrixEndRect` act on arbitrary
1024	free-sheaf morphisms (such as `chartMatrixHom`). -/
1025	lemma matrixEndRect_unitEndSection {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1026	    (φ : SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
1027	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :
1028	    matrixEndRect (Matrix.of fun p j =>
1029	        unitEndSection (SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ φ ≫ projFree p)) = φ := by
1030	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin r)) _ _ (fun j => ?_)
1031	  simp only [SheafOfModules.freeCofan_inj]
1032	  -- compare the two maps `unit ⟶ free (Fin d)` against the biproduct projections
1033	  refine (cancel_mono (biproduct.isoCoproduct
1034	    (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).inv).mp ?_
1035	  refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
1036	  simp only [Category.assoc]

1043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ⟶ M i :=
1044	  ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv _ _).symm

1047	/-- **The chart restriction of the glued sheaf is the equalizer of the restricted
1048	descent legs**: the chart pullback preserves the descent equalizer

1050	`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`. Project-local. -/
1051	noncomputable def glueRestrictEqualizerIso (i : D.J) :
1052	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2)
1053	      ≅ equalizer ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegA D M))
1054	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegB D M g)) :=
1055	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).mapIso (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2) ≪≫
1056	    PreservesEqualizer.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) _ _

1058	/-- **The chart restriction of the pushforward product is the product of the
1059	restrictions**: the chart pullback preserves the product
1060	(`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion`). Second reduction step of
1061	`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`: the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` are then identified with
1062	`(f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` by the overlap base change of the cartesian chart
1063	square (`glueData_preimage_image_eq`). Project-local. -/
1064	noncomputable def glueRestrictProdIso (i : D.J) :
1065	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glueProd D M)
1066	      ≅ ∏ᶜ fun j => (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj
1067	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j)) :=
1068	  PreservesProduct.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) _

1070	/-- **Chart restriction of a lifted morphism**: pulling a `glueLift` back to the `i`-th
1071	chart and composing with the restriction morphism recovers the adjoint transpose of the
1072	`i`-th component of the lifted family. This is `glueLift_glueProj` transposed along the
1073	chart immersion; it is what identifies `ι_I^* (tautologicalQuotient)` with the chart
1074	quotient downstream. Project-local. -/
1075	lemma pullback_map_glueLift_glueRestrictionHom {W : D.glued.Modules}
1076	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
1077	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
1078	      k p.1 ≫
1079	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
1080	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
1081	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
1082	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
1083	        = k p.2 ≫
1084	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
1085	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
1086	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
1087	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
1088	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
1089	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
1090	            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
1091	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr

1095	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLift D M g hC1 hC2 k hk) ≫

1097	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv _ _).symm (k i) := by

input.description: Check abelian instance conditions and whether it's imported

content:
In particular, if `u = v` and `C : Type u` is a small category,
then `SheafOfModules.{u} R` is abelian: this instance shall be
found automatically if this file and `Algebra.Category.Grp.FilteredColimits`
are imported.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v' u u'

open CategoryTheory Limits

variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}

namespace SheafOfModules

variable (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]

noncomputable instance : Abelian (SheafOfModules.{v} R) := by
  let adj := PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)
  exact abelianOfAdjunction _ _ (asIso (adj.counit)) adj

end SheafOfModules

/-- **The local-to-global inverse of the universal property** (Nitsure §1): a rank-`d`
locally free quotient `q : O_T^r ↠ F` determines a morphism `T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` — the gluing,
input.new_string:
/-! ### Chart data of a pulled-back quotient: the locus-level bridge

The two inverse laws of `represents` compare the chart data of `rqPullback ψ y` on `T'`
with the `ψ`-pullback of the chart data of `y` on `T`. `chartComposite_rqPullback` is the
morphism-level bridge; `chartLocus_rqPullback` below is the locus-level bridge. -/

/-- **The chart locus of a pullback contains the preimage of the chart locus**
(`lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback`): where the chart composite of `y` is invertible, the
chart composite of `ψ^*y` is invertible after pulling back. With
`chartComposite_rqPullback` this is the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp` plus
`isIso_pullback_map_comp` along the factorisation of `V.ι ≫ ψ` through the locus
inclusion. Project-local. -/
lemma chartLocus_rqPullback {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) {r d : ℕ}
    (y : RankQuotient r d T) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    ψ ⁻¹ᵁ chartLocus y I hI ≤ chartLocus (rqPullback ψ y) I hI := by
  set V : T'.Opens := ψ ⁻¹ᵁ chartLocus y I hI with hV
  -- the composite `V.ι ≫ ψ` factors through the chart-locus inclusion
  have hrange : Set.range (V.ι ≫ ψ).base ⊆ Set.range (chartLocus y I hI).ι.base := by
    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
    rintro t ⟨v, rfl⟩
    have hv : V.ι.base v ∈ V := by
      rw [← Scheme.Opens.range_ι]; exact ⟨v, rfl⟩
    rw [Scheme.comp_base_apply]
    exact hv
  set ρ : V.toScheme ⟶ (chartLocus y I hI).toScheme :=
    IsOpenImmersion.lift (chartLocus y I hI).ι (V.ι ≫ ψ) hrange with hρ
  have hfac : ρ ≫ (chartLocus y I hI).ι = V.ι ≫ ψ :=
    IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hrange
  -- the chart composite of `y` pulls back to an iso along `V.ι ≫ ψ`
  haveI h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ρ ≫ (chartLocus y I hI).ι)).map
      (chartComposite y I hI)) := isIso_pullback_map_comp _ _ _
  haveI h2 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (V.ι ≫ ψ)).map (chartComposite y I hI)) := by
    rw [← hfac]; exact h1
  -- transport through the pseudofunctor comparison to the iterated pullback
  haveI h3 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map (chartComposite y I hI))) := by
    have h4 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
        (chartComposite y I hI)) :=

/-- The glued sheaf *is* the equalizer of the two (re-exposed) descent legs. The
isomorphism is definitional (`Iso.refl`); it exists to let the equalizer API fire
syntactically on `glue`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueIsoEqualizer :
    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ≅ equalizer (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) :=
  Iso.refl _

/-- Projection of the glued sheaf onto the `i`-th pushforward factor `(ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`:
the equalizer inclusion followed by the product projection. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueProj (i : D.J) :
    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i) :=
  (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2).hom ≫ equalizer.ι (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) ≫
    Pi.π _ i

/-- `glueLift` followed by the `i`-th projection recovers the `i`-th component of the
lifted family. Project-local. -/

input.new_string:
/-! ### The chart data of the tautological quotient

Over the chart immersion `ι_I` the tautological quotient is presented by the universal
matrix: `pullback_map_glueLift_glueRestrictionHom` identifies `ι_I^* u` with the chart
quotient `u^I` (through the chart restriction isomorphism), the `I`-minor of `u^I` is
the identity, and the presented matrix is the `Γ`-image of `X^I`. These are the
taut-specific inputs of the `represents` inverse laws. -/

/-- **The `I`-columns of the chart quotient give a splitting**: `s_I ≫ u^I = 𝟙`
(matrix content: the `I`-minor of the universal matrix is the identity). Standalone form
of the splitting inside `chartQuotientMap_epi`. Project-local. -/
lemma freeMap_chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
  have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
        (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
          chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
    (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
      (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
  exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm

/-- **Chart restriction of the tautological quotient**: pulling the tautological
quotient back along the chart immersion `ι_I` and composing with the chart restriction
isomorphism gives the chart quotient `u^I`, up to the free-pullback comparison —
`pullback_map_glueLift_glueRestrictionHom` at the Grassmannian glue data, with the
adjunction transpose collapsed by `Equiv.symm_apply_apply`. Project-local. -/
lemma pullback_map_tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map (tautologicalQuotient d r) ≫
        (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).hom ≫
        chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2 := by
  have h := Scheme.Modules.pullback_map_glueLift_glueRestrictionHom (theGlueData d r)
    (fun J => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
    (bundleTransitionData d r)
    (fun J => bundleTransition_self d r J.1 J.2)
    (fun J J' K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r J J' K)
    (fun J => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r J)
    (fun p => (tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose d r p.1 p.2).mpr
      (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2)) I
  have h2 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
        ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).homEquiv _ _).symm
        (tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).hom ≫
        chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2 := by
    rw [tautologicalQuotientComponent]
    exact Equiv.symm_apply_apply _ _
  exact h.trans h2

/-- **Chart pullback of the tautological chart composite**: `ι_I^*(s_I ≫ u)` is the
free-pullback comparison followed by the inverse chart restriction isomorphism — the
`I`-minor of the chart quotient is the identity (`freeMap_chartQuotientMap`), so the
chart composite of the tautological quotient trivialises over its own chart.
Project-local. -/
lemma pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
        (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d)).hom ≫
        (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).inv := by
  -- the pulled-back quotient, in restriction-iso form
  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
        ((tautologicalRankQuotient d r).q)
      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).hom ≫
          chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2) ≫
        (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).inv :=
    (Iso.eq_comp_inv _).mpr (pullback_map_tautologicalQuotient d r I)
  -- the pulled-back coordinate inclusion, comparison-intertwined
  have hfm : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
        (SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf)
          (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r)))
      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d)).hom ≫
          SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r))) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).inv :=
    (Iso.eq_comp_inv _).mpr (pullback_map_freeMap_pullbackFreeIso _ _)
  -- split the chart composite and chain the two identifications
  have hsplit : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
        (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf)
            (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r))) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
          ((tautologicalRankQuotient d r).q) :=
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map_comp _ _
  rw [hsplit, hq, hfm]
  -- collapse `E.inv ≫ E.hom` and the splitting `s_I ≫ u^I = 𝟙`
  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  rw [freeMap_chartQuotientMap_assoc d r I.1 I.2]

/-- The tautological chart composite pulls back to an isomorphism along its own chart
immersion — instance form of `pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological`, the input of
the presented-matrix computation `presentedMatrix_tautological`. Project-local. -/
instance isIso_pullback_chartComposite_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
      (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)) := by
  rw [pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological]
  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance inferInstance

/-- **The chart image lies in the tautological chart locus**: the open range of the
chart immersion `ι_I` is one of the opens over which the tautological chart composite
is invertible. Project-local — provides the cover for the uniqueness law of
`represents`. -/
lemma opensRange_le_chartLocus_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    Scheme.Hom.opensRange ((theGlueData d r).ι I)
      ≤ chartLocus (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2 := by
  have heq : ((theGlueData d r).ι I).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I
      = (Scheme.Hom.opensRange ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).ι := by
    rw [← Scheme.Hom.isoOpensRange_hom_ι ((theGlueData d r).ι I), Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  haveI h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
      (((theGlueData d r).ι I).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
      (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)) :=
    isIso_pullback_map_comp _ _ _
  have hmem : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
      (Scheme.Hom.opensRange ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).ι).map
      (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)) := by
    rw [← heq]; exact h1
  exact le_sSup hmem

-- the inverse-collapse steps traverse the `X.Modules` instance diamond
/-- **The tautological quotient is presented by the universal matrix**: over the `I`-th
chart, the matrix presenting the tautological quotient (against its own inverted chart
composite) is the `Γ`-image of the universal matrix `X^I`. The taut-specific layer of
the `represents` inverse laws. Project-local. -/
lemma presentedMatrix_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    presentedMatrix (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) ((theGlueData d r).ι I) I.1 I.2
      = (universalMatrix d r I.1 I.2).map
          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of
            (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ))).inv) := by
  apply matrixEndRect_injective
  rw [matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix, ← chartQuotientMap_eq_matrixEndRect]
  -- the inverse of the pulled-back chart composite, explicitly
  have hinv : @CategoryTheory.inv _ _ _ _
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
          (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2))
        (isIso_pullback_chartComposite_tautological d r I)
      = (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).hom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d)).inv := by
    apply IsIso.inv_eq_of_hom_inv_id
    rw [pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological]
    simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id]
  rw [hinv, hq_taut d r I]
  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc,
    Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]

## Know your target in the dependency graph (leandag)

The blueprint dependency graph is queryable **read-only** — `archon` is on PATH, `--json` is parseable (the banner goes to stderr). Use it to ground your work instead of guessing:

- `archon dag-query node --node <label> --json` — your target's blueprint entry, its `\lean{}` pin, and status. **If the `\lean{}` pin is a `…TODO.…` placeholder, the Lean declaration does not exist yet** — you must *create* it (the typed `sorry`, or the proof if your mode builds it), not "fill" a name that isn't in the environment.
- `archon dag-query ancestors --node <label> --json` — your target's full dependency closure. Build on deps that are actually done (`\leanok`); a dependency still marked ∞ / `sorry` is **not** a sound foundation — cite it only if it genuinely typechecks today.
- `archon dag-query gaps --json` — the ∞ holes (statements with no informal proof). If your target's cone hits one, the blueprint sketch is incomplete: flag it in your task_result rather than inventing a proof to paper over it.
- `archon dag-query unmatched --json` — Lean decls with no blueprint entry. After you add helpers, your new names surface here until they're blueprinted — that is the debt you must report (see Logging → "Needs blueprint entry").

The label↔Lean mapping is the `\lean{}` annotation in the blueprint block; `leandag stats` / `leandag focus` also give a project-wide view. You never *write* the graph — that's the dag/plan/review agents' job — but reading it keeps you from proving against a hollow or unfinished foundation.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any declaration. You may fill proof bodies of protected declarations but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.

## Talking to the user (TO_USER.md)

`.archon/TO_USER.md` is a *persistent* shared notice board surfaced to the user as a banner. You may add a bullet **only** for something the user genuinely must act on that you hit while proving — e.g. a missing credential/dependency a sorry is gated on, or a `/- USER: -/` question you cannot resolve. Discipline:

- **Concise + relevant**: keep the whole file ≤ 2–3 short bullets; before adding, read it and delete any bullet no longer true. Never a question queue — the loop never waits.
- **Concurrency**: provers run in parallel (one per file). To avoid clobbering a sibling lane's bullet, append your single bullet prefixed with your file (`- [Foo/Bar.lean] …`) rather than rewriting the whole file, and only when it's truly user-actionable. Routine "couldn't close this sorry" notes go in `task_results/<your_file>.md`, NOT here.

## Avoid early termination

- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
- **Decompose**: break into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available) and solve each individually.
- **Hard bar is a minimum, not a ceiling.** If your objectives specify a "hard bar" (e.g. "add def + pin signature"), that tells you the minimum required — not where to stop. After meeting it:
  - If a recipe exists in `analogies/`, the blueprint chapter has a concrete proof sketch, or you can reason about an approach: attempt the proof body immediately.
  - If no recipe exists: formulate a strategy, attempt it, and leave a partial tactic block at the stuck point. A failed attempt with a `by ... sorry` is far more useful than a clean `sorry`.
  - A bare `sorry` with no attempt is only acceptable when the mathematical obstacle is explicitly named and at least one concrete approach has been tried and documented.

Any `-- TODO: try X`, `-- could use Y here`, or `/- Next step: ... -/` you write is a sign you stopped too early — attempt X/Y first; only leave the comment if the attempt fails with a named error. The same applies to approaches proposed in task results: if you wrote it in your log as "a possible route", you should have tried it.

## When to stop

Stop only when progress is blocked by a specific, named obstacle in one of these categories:

1. A genuinely missing Mathlib ingredient — named precisely, attempted via informal agent, no workaround found.
2. A definition or signature that requires another prover's output — name the specific declaration and file.
3. A proof that requires fundamental restructuring of a protected or cross-file definition — name it.

"It seems hard", "the proof is complex", "I couldn't find the right lemma", or "the planner should weigh in on this approach" are NOT valid stop reasons. Document the specific obstacle and keep trying. Only stop when you can write: "I tried approaches X, Y, Z; each fails because [specific mathematical reason]; the informal agent suggested [route] which requires [specific missing ingredient]."

**Specifically banned stop phrases:**
- "I'll wait for the planner to decide."
- "This strategy should be confirmed by the next iteration."
- "I'll defer this choice to the planner."
- "The planner may want to reconsider the approach."

If you find yourself writing any of these: delete the sentence, pick the most promising route, and attempt it. The planner will correct the choice next iter if it was wrong — a failed attempt costs nothing compared to an idle session.

- **`Classical.choice` around an explicit witness** — dissolving `Type` vs `Prop` friction without actually constructing the witness.
- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`** — discards the declaration post-elaboration, breaking blueprint cross-references.

**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, ship the typed `sorry` instead.

## When infrastructure is missing

Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before filing "Infrastructure missing":

1. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 2.
2. **If the missing ingredient is ≤~100 LOC**: write it as a project-local helper — typed signature plus a genuine proof attempt. A partial proof body is better than a documented gap.
3. **Only after steps 1–2 are exhausted**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` with what you tried, why it failed, and the precise statement of the missing ingredient for the plan agent to assign in `mathlib-build` mode.

"Infrastructure missing" as a verdict requires: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), local helper attempted if in scope. "I don't see how" is not evidence — a failed attempt with `sorry` at the stuck step IS evidence.

When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.

**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

## Proof style

- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **List every new declaration you introduce under a `## Needs blueprint entry` heading in your task_result** (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) — see Logging below. The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: the review agent and planner consume this list (and `archon dag-query unmatched`) to give each new declaration a blueprint block. An unreported helper is an invisible, isolated dependency that silently corrupts the frontier — flagging it is mandatory, not optional. (You never write the blueprint yourself; you only name what needs one.)
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap

## Needs blueprint entry
- `Algebra.WLocal.helper_bijective` (line 78) — new helper, no blueprint block yet. Uses: `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective`. Reviewer/planner: add a `\label` + `\lean` + `\uses` entry so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results. **Always include the `## Needs blueprint entry` section when you added any new declaration** (omit it only if you added none) — it is how the loop keeps Lean ↔ blueprint at 1-to-1.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

**Process lifetime discipline:** A prover lane stops by writing `task_results/<your_file>.md` and exiting normally. Do NOT run `pkill`, `killall`, `kill`, regex process cleanup, or any command intended to terminate Lean/Lake/shell/agent processes. Do not launch final verification in the background and then clean it up. Verification must be a blocking command whose result you wait for, or a clearly reported unfinished command. The runner/orchestrator owns cleanup after your report.

Before writing results:

1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles.
3. **Write a `## Summary` section** stating: sorry count before → after; exact names of sorries closed; exact names of sorries still open and why; whether you attempted adjacent sorries beyond the assigned ones.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.
   - `Infrastructure missing`: a specific Mathlib gap was the blocker — name it precisely, confirm the informal agent was called (or key unavailable), describe what it suggested, why it failed, and state the exact missing ingredient statement.
   - `Directive not followed`: explain which part of the planner's directive you deviated from and why.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.

126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

2	
3	## isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map (was L2160) — **RESOLVED**
4	### Attempt 1
5	- **Approach:** Blueprint's stalk-wise route, entirely on Mathlib API: each point of the
6	  iso-locus has a neighbourhood `U` with `φ|_U` invertible; `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso`
7	  shows restriction along an open immersion preserves abelian-presheaf stalks, so the stalk of
8	  `φ` is invertible at every locus point; the same comparison for the locus inclusion makes the
9	  restriction stalkwise invertible; `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` upgrades to
10	  an isomorphism of `Ab`-sheaves; `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` reflects isomorphisms;
11	  `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` transports `restrictFunctor` to `pullback`.
12	- **Result:** RESOLVED (~60 lines, no new sorries, verified by cold `lake build`).
13	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor` + `restrictStalkNatIso`
14	  (AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean) make the stalk argument essentially free — no
15	  module-sheaf stalk theory needed, only `Ab`-presheaf stalks.
16	- **Note:** the previously `sorry`-backed instance `isIso_pullback_chartLocus_map` and all the
17	  `chartMatrixHom`/`chartMatrix`/`chartMorphism` machinery downstream are now sorry-free.
18	
19	## chartLocus_isOpenCover (was L2147) — **RESOLVED**
20	### Attempt 1 (the Nakayama covering step, Nitsure §1)
21	- **Approach:** New pipeline, replacing the blueprint's stalkwise-surjectivity sketch with an
22	  affine-local splitting argument that needs NO stalk theory for sheaves of modules (which the
23	  SNAP notes record as ABSENT):
24	  1. **Epi between free sheaves on `Spec R` splits** (`exists_section_of_epi_free_spec`):
25	     conjugate through `AlgebraicGeometry.tildeFinsupp : tilde (ι →₀ R) ≅ free ι`; the fully
26	     faithful `tilde.functor` reflects the epi to `ModuleCat R` where epi = surjective
27	     (`ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective`); `Module.projective_lifting_property` (free ⇒ projective)
28	     gives a module-level section; transport back through `tilde.map`.
29	     **Defeq check that makes this work: `CommRingCat.of ↥R = R` is `rfl`,** so
30	     `tildeFinsupp` applies literally over `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf`.
31	  2. **Matrix right inverse over `Spec R`** (`exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec`):
32	     present the section by `matrixEndRect_unitEndSection`, compose with the new fully
33	     rectangular law `matrixEndRect_comp_rect`, conclude `M * G = 1` by the new
34	     `matrixEndRect_injective`.
35	  3. **Affine transport** (`exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect`): conjugate along
36	     `S.isoSpec.inv` with `pullback_conj_matrixEndRect`; pull the right inverse back along the
37	     ring iso `appTop`.
38	  4. **Pointwise (Nitsure):** trivialise `x.F` on an affine `W ∋ t` (affine-opens basis refining
39	     the local-freeness cover), present `ι_W^* x.q` by a matrix `MW` (epi: pullback is a left
40	     adjoint), get `MW * G = 1`; evaluate at the residue field of the stalk at `t`
41	     (`χ := residue ∘ germ`); `exists_isUnit_submatrix` (new, pure linear algebra:
42	     columns span by right inverse, `exists_linearIndependent`, basis cardinality,
43	     `Matrix.linearIndependent_cols_iff_isUnit`) gives a size-`d` subset `I` with invertible
44	     residue minor; the minor determinant `f0` has unit germ (residue ≠ 0 ⇒ outside the maximal
45	     ideal), so `t ∈ W.basicOpen f0`; on that basic open the minor matrix is invertible
46	     (`RingedSpace.isUnit_res_basicOpen` + `Matrix.nonsing_inv`), so the chart composite —
47	     identified chart-locally with `matrixEndRect (MW.submatrix id σ_I)` via
48	     `pullback_map_freeMap_pullbackFreeIso` and the new `freeMap_matrixEndRect` — pulls back to
49	     an isomorphism; transport along `(Wb.ι ≫ W.ι).opensRange` factorisation into
50	     `mem_isoLocus`.
51	- **Result:** RESOLVED (~600 lines incl. helpers, verified by cold `lake build`, 0 axioms).
52	- **Dead ends / warnings for future provers:**
53	  - `Epi → IsLocallySurjective` for `SheafOfModules` does NOT exist in Mathlib and the
54	    presheaf-image/balanced route is expensive — the tilde splitting above is the cheap
55	    substitute on affines; reuse it.
56	  - `simp only [Category.assoc]` and `rw` of `CommRingCat.hom_comp` FAIL silently across the
57	    `X.Modules`/`SheafOfModules` instance diamond in several spots (consistent with
58	    `gr-pullback-instance-diamond`); the fixes were term-mode assoc chains, `show` for
59	    rfl-equalities of ring-hom applications, and `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (… ≪≫ …).hom)` with
60	    explicitly assembled isos instead of `infer_instance` on compositions.
61	  - `matrixEnd M = matrixEndRect M` is `rfl` for square `M` (`matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect`), and
62	    `Matrix.submatrix_map` is `rfl` — used to make `matrixToFreeIso` apply definitionally where
63	    instance search across the diamond fails.
64	  - `Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd` returns `TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥T`, not `T.Opens` — re-obtain
65	    through an ascribed existential or dot-notation (`W.toScheme`, `W.ι`) fails.
66	
67	## grPointOfRankQuotient overlap compatibility (sorry at ~L3180 after edits) — PARTIAL
68	- **State:** still `sorry`, but the matrix heart of the proof is now FORMALIZED and compiling:
69	  - `pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap` — inverse form of the index-map intertwiner.
70	  - `presentedMatrix x j I hI` — the presented matrix of the quotient along any `j : P ⟶ T`
71	    where `j^* c_I` is invertible (generalises `chartMatrix`, which is the case
72	    `j = (chartLocus x I hI).ι`).
73	  - `matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix`, `matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor` — the presentation
74	    identities (the `J`-minor presents `j^*c_J` against `(j^*c_I)⁻¹`).
75	  - `presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis` — **Nitsure's `M^I = M^I_J · M^J`** over any such `j`.
76	  - `isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect` — converse matrix-unit bridge; with
77	    `matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor` (whose right side is a composite of isos when both
78	    chart composites are invertible) it yields `IsUnit (M^I_P)_J`, hence
79	    `IsUnit (det (M^I_P)_J)` via `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det` — exactly the unit needed for
80	    the `IsLocalization.Away.lift` step.
81	- **Remaining for the sorry itself (steps 1, 4, 5 of the route below):**
82	  1. Both sides are `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (classifying)` composites: `p1 ≫ chartMorphism I =
83	     P.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (classifying_I ≫ appTop p1)` by `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality`
84	     (exact pattern of the proven `chartMorphism_rel`).
85	  2. **Change-of-basis matrix identity over the intersection `P`** (the jewel): with both chart
86	     composites invertible after pullback to `P`, the presented matrices satisfy
87	     `M^I_P = (M^I_P)_J * M^J_P`, by the morphism computation
88	     `matrixEndRect (M^J_P) ≫ matrixEnd ((M^I_P).submatrix id σ_J) =
89	      [Qr.inv ≫ q ≫ (c_J)⁻¹ ≫ Qd.hom] ≫ [Qd.inv ≫ c_J ≫ (c_I)⁻¹ ≫ Qd.hom]
90	      = matrixEndRect (M^I_P)` + `matrixEndRect_comp` + `matrixEndRect_injective`
91	     (all three now in-file). The J-minor identification
92	     `freeMap σ_J ≫ matrixEndRect M^I_P = Qd.inv ≫ (pb)c_J ≫ inv((pb)c_I) ≫ Qd.hom`
93	     follows from `pullback_map_freeMap_pullbackFreeIso` + `(pb)(freeMap σ_J) ≫ (pb)q = (pb)c_J`.
94	  3. `IsUnit (det (M^I_P)_J)`: the minor's `matrixEndRect` is the iso of step 2; present its
95	     inverse (`matrixEndRect_unitEndSection`) and use `matrixEndRect_comp_rect` + injectivity to
96	     get a two-sided matrix inverse, then `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det`.
97	  4. Factor `p1 ≫ chartMorphism I` through `chartIncl` (= the away-localisation `Spec.map`):
98	     `IsLocalization.Away.lift` at `P^I_J ↦ det (M^I_P)_J` (unit by step 3); equality of the
99	     two factorisations after `chartTransition` reduces by `Spec.map`-functoriality +
100	     `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext` + `MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext` to step 2's matrix identity
101	     against `universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` (GrassmannianCells, proven).
102	  5. Conclude with `Scheme.GlueData.glue_condition`.

104	  style) + GrassmannianCells API archaeology (`chartIncl`/`chartTransition`/`transitionPreMap`

4562	/-! ### Chart data of a pulled-back quotient: the locus-level bridge
4563	
4564	The two inverse laws of `represents` compare the chart data of `rqPullback ψ y` on `T'`
4565	with the `ψ`-pullback of the chart data of `y` on `T`. `chartComposite_rqPullback` is the
