4422	/-- **Chart pullback of the tautological chart composite**: `ι_I^*(s_I ≫ u)` is the
4423	free-pullback comparison followed by the inverse chart restriction isomorphism — the
4424	`I`-minor of the chart quotient is the identity (`freeMap_chartQuotientMap`), so the
4425	chart composite of the tautological quotient trivialises over its own chart.
4426	Project-local. -/
4427	lemma pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
4428	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4429	        (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)
4430	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d)).hom ≫
4431	        (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).inv := by
4432	  -- the pulled-back quotient, in restriction-iso form
4433	  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4434	        ((tautologicalRankQuotient d r).q)
4435	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).hom ≫
4436	          chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2) ≫
4437	        (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).inv :=
4438	    (Iso.eq_comp_inv _).mpr (pullback_map_tautologicalQuotient d r I)
4439	  -- the pulled-back coordinate inclusion, comparison-intertwined
4440	  have hfm : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4441	        (SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf)
4442	          (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r)))
4443	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d)).hom ≫
4444	          SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r))) ≫
4445	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).inv :=
4446	    (Iso.eq_comp_inv _).mpr (pullback_map_freeMap_pullbackFreeIso _ _)
4447	  -- split the chart composite and chain the two identifications
4448	  have hsplit : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4449	        (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)
4450	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4451	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf)
4452	            (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r))) ≫
4453	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4454	          ((tautologicalRankQuotient d r).q) :=
4455	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map_comp _ _
4456	  rw [hsplit, hq, hfm]
4457	  -- collapse `E.inv ≫ E.hom` and the splitting `s_I ≫ u^I = 𝟙` (term-mode: positional
4458	  -- `simp [Category.assoc]` does not reassociate across the `X.Modules` diamond)
4459	  set E := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r) with hE
4460	  set Dd := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d) with hDd
4461	  set Φ := universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I with hΦ
4462	  set cqm := chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2 with hcqm
4463	  set fm : SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
4464	      SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) :=
4465	    SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => ((I.1).orderIsoOfFin I.2 j : Fin r)) with hfm'
4466	  have hsplit' : fm ≫ cqm = 𝟙 _ := freeMap_chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2
4467	  calc ((Dd.hom ≫ fm) ≫ E.inv) ≫ (E.hom ≫ cqm) ≫ Φ.inv
4468	      = (Dd.hom ≫ fm) ≫ E.inv ≫ (E.hom ≫ cqm) ≫ Φ.inv := Category.assoc _ _ _
4469	    _ = (Dd.hom ≫ fm) ≫ E.inv ≫ E.hom ≫ cqm ≫ Φ.inv :=
4470	        congrArg ((Dd.hom ≫ fm) ≫ ·) (congrArg (E.inv ≫ ·) (Category.assoc _ _ _))
4471	    _ = (Dd.hom ≫ fm) ≫ cqm ≫ Φ.inv :=
4472	        congrArg ((Dd.hom ≫ fm) ≫ ·) (Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc E (cqm ≫ Φ.inv))
4473	    _ = Dd.hom ≫ fm ≫ cqm ≫ Φ.inv := Category.assoc _ _ _
4474	    _ = Dd.hom ≫ (fm ≫ cqm) ≫ Φ.inv :=
4475	        congrArg (Dd.hom ≫ ·) (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
4476	    _ = Dd.hom ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ Φ.inv :=
4477	        congrArg (Dd.hom ≫ ·) (congrArg (· ≫ Φ.inv) hsplit')
4478	    _ = Dd.hom ≫ Φ.inv := congrArg (Dd.hom ≫ ·) (Category.id_comp _)
4479	
4480	/-- The tautological chart composite pulls back to an isomorphism along its own chart
4481	immersion — instance form of `pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological`, the input of
4482	the presented-matrix computation `presentedMatrix_tautological`. Project-local. -/
4483	instance isIso_pullback_chartComposite_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
4484	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4485	      (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)) := by
4486	  rw [pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological]
4487	  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance inferInstance
4488	
4489	/-- **The chart image lies in the tautological chart locus**: the open range of the
4490	chart immersion `ι_I` is one of the opens over which the tautological chart composite
4491	is invertible. Project-local — provides the cover for the uniqueness law of
4492	`represents`. -/
4493	lemma opensRange_le_chartLocus_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
4494	    Scheme.Hom.opensRange ((theGlueData d r).ι I)
4495	      ≤ chartLocus (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2 := by
4496	  have heq : ((theGlueData d r).ι I).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I
4497	      = (Scheme.Hom.opensRange ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).ι :=
4498	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mpr (Scheme.Hom.isoOpensRange_hom_ι ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).symm
4499	  haveI h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
4500	      (((theGlueData d r).ι I).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4501	      (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)) :=
4502	    isIso_pullback_map_comp _ _ _
4503	  have hmem : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
4504	      (Scheme.Hom.opensRange ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).ι).map
4505	      (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2)) := by
4506	    rw [← heq]; exact h1
4507	  exact le_sSup hmem
4508	
4509	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
4510	-- the inverse-collapse steps traverse the `X.Modules` instance diamond
4511	/-- **The tautological quotient is presented by the universal matrix**: over the `I`-th
4512	chart, the matrix presenting the tautological quotient (against its own inverted chart
4513	composite) is the `Γ`-image of the universal matrix `X^I`. The taut-specific layer of
4514	the `represents` inverse laws. Project-local. -/
4515	lemma presentedMatrix_tautological (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
4516	    presentedMatrix (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) ((theGlueData d r).ι I) I.1 I.2
4517	        (hcI := isIso_pullback_chartComposite_tautological d r I)
4518	      = (universalMatrix d r I.1 I.2).map
4519	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of
4520	            (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ))).inv) := by
4521	  apply matrixEndRect_injective
4522	  letI E := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)
4523	  letI Dd := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin d)
4524	  letI Φ := universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I
4525	  letI cqm := chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2
4526	  -- the inverse of the pulled-back chart composite, explicitly
4527	  have hinv : @CategoryTheory.inv _ _ _ _
4528	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4529	          (chartComposite (tautologicalRankQuotient d r) I.1 I.2))
4530	        (isIso_pullback_chartComposite_tautological d r I)
4531	      = Φ.hom ≫ Dd.inv := by
4532	    apply IsIso.inv_eq_of_hom_inv_id
4533	    -- `(Dd.hom ≫ Φ.inv) ≫ Φ.hom ≫ Dd.inv = 𝟙` (term-mode under the diamond)
4534	    exact (congrArg (· ≫ (Φ.hom ≫ Dd.inv))
4535	        (pullback_map_chartComposite_tautological d r I)).trans
4536	      ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
4537	        ((congrArg (Dd.hom ≫ ·) (Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc Φ Dd.inv)).trans Dd.hom_inv_id))
4538	  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4539	        ((tautologicalRankQuotient d r).q)
4540	      = (E.hom ≫ cqm) ≫ Φ.inv :=
4541	    (Iso.eq_comp_inv _).mpr (pullback_map_tautologicalQuotient d r I)
4542	  have hBC : (Φ.hom ≫ Dd.inv) ≫ Dd.hom = Φ.hom :=
4543	    (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
4544	      ((congrArg (Φ.hom ≫ ·) Dd.inv_hom_id).trans (Category.comp_id _))
4545	  have hA : ((E.hom ≫ cqm) ≫ Φ.inv) ≫ Φ.hom = E.hom ≫ cqm :=
4546	    (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
4547	      ((congrArg ((E.hom ≫ cqm) ≫ ·) Φ.inv_hom_id).trans (Category.comp_id _))
4548	  -- assemble entirely by `.trans` (junction unification is up-to-defeq, which crosses
4549	  -- the hidden-instance mismatches that block `rw`/`set` here)
4550	  exact (matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix (tautologicalRankQuotient d r)
4551	      ((theGlueData d r).ι I) I.1 I.2
4552	      (hcI := isIso_pullback_chartComposite_tautological d r I)).trans
4553	    ((congrArg (fun z => E.inv ≫
4554	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).map
4555	          ((tautologicalRankQuotient d r).q) ≫ z ≫ Dd.hom) hinv).trans
4556	      ((congrArg (fun z => E.inv ≫ z ≫ (Φ.hom ≫ Dd.inv) ≫ Dd.hom) hq).trans
4557	        ((congrArg (fun z => E.inv ≫ ((E.hom ≫ cqm) ≫ Φ.inv) ≫ z) hBC).trans
4558	          ((congrArg (E.inv ≫ ·) hA).trans
4559	            ((Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc E cqm).trans
4560	              (chartQuotientMap_eq_matrixEndRect d r I.1 I.2))))))
4561	
4562	/-! ### Chart data of a pulled-back quotient: the locus-level bridge
4563	
4564	The two inverse laws of `represents` compare the chart data of `rqPullback ψ y` on `T'`
4565	with the `ψ`-pullback of the chart data of `y` on `T`. `chartComposite_rqPullback` is the
4566	morphism-level bridge; `chartLocus_rqPullback` below is the locus-level bridge. -/
4567	
4568	/-- **The chart locus of a pullback contains the preimage of the chart locus**
4569	(`lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback`): where the chart composite of `y` is invertible, the
4570	chart composite of `ψ^*y` is invertible after pulling back. With
4571	`chartComposite_rqPullback` this is the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp` plus
4572	`isIso_pullback_map_comp` along the factorisation of `V.ι ≫ ψ` through the locus
4573	inclusion. Project-local. -/
4574	lemma chartLocus_rqPullback {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) {r d : ℕ}
4575	    (y : RankQuotient r d T) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
4576	    ψ ⁻¹ᵁ chartLocus y I hI ≤ chartLocus (rqPullback ψ y) I hI := by
4577	  set V : T'.Opens := ψ ⁻¹ᵁ chartLocus y I hI with hV
4578	  -- the composite `V.ι ≫ ψ` factors through the chart-locus inclusion
4579	  have hrange : Set.range (V.ι ≫ ψ).base ⊆ Set.range (chartLocus y I hI).ι.base := by
4580	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
4581	    rintro t ⟨v, rfl⟩
4582	    have hv : V.ι.base v ∈ V := by
4583	      have : V.ι.base v ∈ Set.range V.ι.base := ⟨v, rfl⟩
4584	      rwa [Scheme.Opens.range_ι] at this
4585	    show (V.ι ≫ ψ).base v ∈ (chartLocus y I hI : Set T)
4586	    rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
4587	    exact hv
4588	  set ρ : V.toScheme ⟶ (chartLocus y I hI).toScheme :=
4589	    IsOpenImmersion.lift (chartLocus y I hI).ι (V.ι ≫ ψ) hrange with hρ
4590	  have hfac : ρ ≫ (chartLocus y I hI).ι = V.ι ≫ ψ :=
4591	    IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hrange
4592	  -- the chart composite of `y` pulls back to an iso along `V.ι ≫ ψ`
4593	  haveI h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ρ ≫ (chartLocus y I hI).ι)).map
4594	      (chartComposite y I hI)) := isIso_pullback_map_comp _ _ _
4595	  haveI h2 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (V.ι ≫ ψ)).map (chartComposite y I hI)) := by
4596	    rw [← hfac]; exact h1
4597	  -- transport through the pseudofunctor comparison to the iterated pullback
4598	  haveI h3 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
4599	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map (chartComposite y I hI))) := by
4600	    have h4 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
4601	        (chartComposite y I hI)) :=
4602	      (NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι ψ) _).mpr h2
4603	    exact h4
4604	  -- conclude: the chart composite of the pullback is iso over `V`
4605	  have hmem : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
4606	      (chartComposite (rqPullback ψ y) I hI)) := by
4607	    have hsplit : (Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map (chartComposite (rqPullback ψ y) I hI)
4608	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
4609	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin d)).inv ≫
4610	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
4611	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map (chartComposite y I hI)) :=
4612	      (congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map)
4613	        (chartComposite_rqPullback ψ y I hI)).trans
4614	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map_comp _ _)
4615	    rw [hsplit]
4616	    exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance h3
4617	  exact le_sSup hmem
4618	
4619	/-- **The local-to-global inverse of the universal property** (Nitsure §1): a rank-`d`
4620	locally free quotient `q : O_T^r ↠ F` determines a morphism `T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` — the gluing,
4621	over the open cover `{T_I}` of the chart loci (`chartLocus_isOpenCover`), of the
4622	composites `T_I ⟶ U^I ⟶ Gr(d,r)` of the chart morphisms (`chartMorphism`) with the
4623	glue-data immersions; the overlap compatibility is `chartMorphism_glue_compat`. -/
4624	noncomputable def grPointOfRankQuotient {T : Scheme.{0}} (d r : ℕ)
4625	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : T ⟶ scheme d r :=
4626	  (T.openCoverOfIsOpenCover _ (chartLocus_isOpenCover d r x)).glueMorphisms
4627	    (fun I => chartMorphism d r x I.1 I.2 ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I)
4628	    (fun I J => chartMorphism_glue_compat d r x I J)
4629	
4630	/-- The inverse construction is constant on equivalence classes of quotients: an
4631	isomorphism of targets commuting with the quotient maps induces the same chart loci
4632	(`chartLocus_rel`), the same presenting matrices (`chartMatrixHom` is unchanged since
4633	`q_y ≫ (c_y)⁻¹ = q_x ≫ f ≫ f⁻¹ ≫ (c_x)⁻¹ = q_x ≫ (c_x)⁻¹` after `chartComposite_rel`),
4634	hence the same chart morphisms and the same glued morphism (compare both gluings over
4635	the common cover via `Scheme.OpenCover.hom_ext` + `ι_glueMorphisms`). The remaining
4636	content is the transport of `chartMatrix` along the locus equality. -/
4637	lemma grPointOfRankQuotient_rel {T : Scheme.{0}} (d r : ℕ)
4638	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
4639	    grPointOfRankQuotient d r x = grPointOfRankQuotient d r y := by
4640	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
4641	  refine (T.openCoverOfIsOpenCover _ (chartLocus_isOpenCover d r x)).hom_ext _ _ (fun I => ?_)
4642	  have hL : chartLocus x I.1 I.2 = chartLocus y I.1 I.2 := chartLocus_rel ⟨f, hf⟩ I.1 I.2
4643	  -- the defining property of the two glued morphisms on their own chart loci
4644	  have hx : (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r x
4645	      = chartMorphism d r x I.1 I.2 ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by
4646	    rw [grPointOfRankQuotient]
4647	    exact Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms _ _ _ I
4648	  have hy : (chartLocus y I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r y
4649	      = chartMorphism d r y I.1 I.2 ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by
4650	    rw [grPointOfRankQuotient]
4651	    -- the cover must be given explicitly: the index `I` carries the `x`-cover's `I₀`,
4652	    -- which would otherwise steer unification to the wrong cover
4653	    exact Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms
4654	      (T.openCoverOfIsOpenCover _ (chartLocus_isOpenCover d r y)) _ _ I
4655	  -- the cover map of the `x`-cover factors through the (equal) `y`-locus
4656	  have hι : (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι
4657	      = T.homOfLE hL.le ≫ (chartLocus y I.1 I.2).ι := (T.homOfLE_ι hL.le).symm
4658	  change (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r x
4659	    = (chartLocus x I.1 I.2).ι ≫ grPointOfRankQuotient d r y
4660	  rw [hx, chartMorphism_rel d r f hf I.1 I.2 hL.le, hι, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, hy]
4661	  rfl
4662	
4663	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
4664	the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
4665	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
4666	
4667	The forward map sends `ψ : T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` to the pullback `ψ^*(U, u)` of the tautological
4668	pair (`tautologicalRankQuotient`); naturality (`homEquiv_comp`) is the already-proven
4669	pseudofunctoriality `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at the tautological point. The
4670	inverse is the chart-by-chart construction `grPointOfRankQuotient`; the two inverse laws
4671	are the remaining content (they consume the chart restriction isomorphisms
4672	`universalQuotient_restrictionIso` and the glued-scheme universal property). -/
4673	noncomputable def represents (d r : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
4674	    (functor d r).RepresentableBy (scheme d r) where
4675	  homEquiv {T} :=
4676	    { toFun := fun ψ => Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback ψ (tautologicalRankQuotient d r))
4677	      invFun := Quotient.lift (grPointOfRankQuotient d r)
4678	        (fun _ _ h => grPointOfRankQuotient_rel d r h)
4679	      left_inv := fun ψ => by
4680	        -- `grPointOfRankQuotient (ψ^* (U, u)) = ψ`: chart-locally, the pulled-back
4681	        -- matrix of sections is the `ψ`-image of the universal one, so the glued
4682	        -- morphism is `ψ` by uniqueness in the glueMorphisms universal property.
4683	        sorry
4684	      right_inv := fun q => by
4685	        -- `(grPointOfRankQuotient x)^* (U, u) ~ x`: the chart restriction isomorphisms
4686	        -- identify the pullback of the universal pair with `x` chart by chart; the
4687	        -- identifications agree on overlaps, hence glue (sheaf-condition of `Hom`).
4688	        sorry }
4689	  homEquiv_comp {T T'} f g := by
4690	    -- pseudofunctoriality of `rqPullback` at the tautological point — this is
4691	    -- `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at `⟦(U, u)⟧`
4692	    have h := congrArg (fun m => m (Quotient.mk _ (tautologicalRankQuotient d r)))
4693	      ((functor d r).map_comp g.op f.op)
4694	    exact h
4695

2601	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) returns \(M\):
2602	  \(\mathrm{aeval}(M)(X^I) = M\).

2604	\begin{proof}
2605	  \uses{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}
2606	  Entrywise. On a complementary (free) entry \(x^I_{p,q}\) with \(q \notin I\),
2607	  \(\mathrm{aeval}(M)\) returns the corresponding entry of \(M\) by the variable-evaluation
2608	  rule. On an \(I\)-column entry the universal matrix has the identity minor, so the value
2609	  there is the corresponding entry of \(1_{d\times d}\), which matches the \(I\)-minor of
2610	  \(M\) -- the identity by \cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}.

2613	\begin{lemma}[Transport of the image matrix along a ring homomorphism]
2614	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom}
2615	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_map_ringHom}
2616	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_universal_matrix, lem:gr_minorDet_unit,
2617	    lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}
2618	  For size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\) and any ring homomorphism
2619	  \(\mathrm{incl} : R^I_J \to D\) out of the localised overlap ring whose composite with
2620	  the structure map \(R^I \to R^I_J\) equals \(g\), pushing the image matrix
2621	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) forward along \(\mathrm{incl}\) yields \(\bigl((g_{*}X^I)_J\bigr)^{-1}
2622	  (g_{*}X^I)\), where \(g_{*}X^I\) is \(X^I\) base-changed along \(g\):
2623	  \[
2624	    \mathrm{incl}_{*}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)
2625	      \;=\; \bigl((g_{*}X^I)_J\bigr)^{-1}\, (g_{*}X^I).
2626	  \]
2627	\end{lemma}
2628	\begin{proof}
2629	  \uses{lem:gr_minorDet_unit, lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}
2630	  The image matrix is the product of the Cramer inverse \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\)
2631	  (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}) with \(X^I\); ring homomorphisms commute with matrix
2632	  multiplication, with the submatrix (minor) operation, and -- because the minor
2633	  determinant \(P^I_J\) is a unit (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}) -- with the nonsingular
2634	  inverse (\cref{lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}). Pushing each factor forward along
2635	  \(\mathrm{incl}\) and rewriting the structure-map composite as \(g\) gives the stated
2636	  base-changed expression.
2637	\end{proof}
2638	
2639	\begin{lemma}[Naturality of the chart morphism]
2640	  \label{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism}
2641	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.comp_chartMorphism}
2642	  \uses{def:gr_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix,
2643	    lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}
2644	  For \(p : W \to T_I\) on which the pulled-back \(I\)-chart composite is invertible, the
2645	  composite \(p \circ \varphi_I : W \to U^I\) is the morphism classified by the matrix
2646	  presenting the quotient along \(p \circ \iota_{T_I}\) -- i.e.\ classified by
2647	  \(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, p \circ \iota_{T_I}, I)\).
2648	\end{lemma}
2649	\begin{proof}
2650	  \uses{lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}
2651	  Naturality of the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\) classifying map turns \(p \circ \varphi_I\) into
2652	  the morphism classified by the \(p\)-restriction of the chart matrix. The chart matrix is
2653	  the presented matrix along \(\iota_{T_I}\) (\cref{lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix}),
2654	  and its \(p\)-restriction is the presented matrix along \(p \circ \iota_{T_I}\)
2655	  (\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}); the two classifying maps therefore coincide.
2656	\end{proof}
2657	
2658	\begin{lemma}[Chart-point identity over a commutative ring]
2659	  \label{lem:gr_chart_point_eq}
2660	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chart_point_eq}
2661	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
2662	  Let \(K\) be a commutative ring and \(f : R^I \to K\) a ring map under which the minor
2663	  \(P^I_J\) is a unit. Then the transported classifying map
2664	  \(\mathrm{lift}(f) \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}\) presents the same \(K\)-point through chart
2665	  \(J\) as \(f\) does through chart \(I\):
2666	  \[
2667	    \Spec(\mathrm{lift}(f) \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}) \circ \iota_J
2668	      \;=\; \Spec(f) \circ \iota_I.
2669	  \]
2670	\end{lemma}
2671	\begin{proof}
2672	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
2673	  Factor \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) as the chart transition followed by the chart inclusion
2674	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}); the glue condition of the glue data
2675	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) makes the transition square commute, and the localisation
2676	  lift recovers \(f\) after the chart inclusion. Assembling the two reductions identifies
2677	  the two composites into the glued scheme.
2678	\end{proof}
2679	
2680	\begin{lemma}[Overlap compatibility of the chart morphisms]
2681	  \label{lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2682	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2683	  \uses{def:gr_chartMorphism, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2684	    lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism, lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis,
2685	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom,
2686	    lem:gr_chart_point_eq, lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect,
2687	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
2688	  On the intersection \(P = T_I \times_T T_J\) the two composites of the chart morphisms
2689	  with the glue-data immersions agree:
2690	  \[
2691	    \mathrm{pr}_1 \circ (\varphi_I \circ \iota_I)
2692	      \;=\; \mathrm{pr}_2 \circ (\varphi_J \circ \iota_J).
2693	  \]
2694	  This is the gluing hypothesis from which \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x)\) is
2695	  assembled.
2696	\end{lemma}
2697	\begin{proof}
2698	  \uses{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp, lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism,
2699	    lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis, lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix,
2700	    lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom, lem:gr_chart_point_eq,
2701	    lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
2702	  On \(P\) both chart composites pull back to isomorphisms
2703	  (\cref{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}), so the pulled-back quotient is presented by both
2704	  matrices \(M^I_P\) and \(M^J_P\); through \cref{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism} the two
2705	  composites are classified by these matrices. The \(J\)-minor of \(M^I_P\) is invertible
2706	  (\cref{lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect},
2707	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}), and the change-of-basis identity
2708	  \(M^I_P = (M^I_P)_J \cdot M^J_P\) (\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis}) --
2709	  realised on the universal matrices via
2710	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix} and the image-matrix transport
2711	  \cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom} -- identifies the \(J\)-classifying ring map with
2712	  the localisation-transport \(\mathrm{lift}(\mathrm{aeval}\,M^I_P) \circ
2713	  \tilde\theta_{I,J}\) of the \(I\)-classifying map. The glue-condition identity
2714	  (\cref{lem:gr_chart_point_eq}) then closes the square into the glued scheme.
2715	\end{proof}

2717	\begin{definition}\leanok
2718	[The morphism induced by a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2719	  \label{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}
2720	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.grPointOfRankQuotient}
2721	  \uses{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map,
2722	    def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle,
2723	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chartComposite, def:gr_chartMatrix,
2724	    def:gr_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2725	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
2726	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) on a scheme \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}). The morphism
2727	  \[
2728	    \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x) \;:\; T \;\longrightarrow\; \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
2729	  \]
2730	  is constructed as follows. Over each chart locus \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\)
2731	  (\cref{def:chartLocus}) the chart composite \(s_I \circ q\) is an isomorphism
2732	  (\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}), so trivialising \(\mathcal{F}|_{T_I}\) by its
2733	  inverse presents \(q|_{T_I}\) as a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) of global functions on
2734	  \(T_I\) whose \(I\)-minor is the identity. Its \(d(r-d)\) complementary entries determine a
2735	  unique \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to \Gamma(T_I,
2736	  \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\), \(x^I_{p,q} \mapsto M^I_{p,q}\), hence by the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\)
2737	  adjunction a unique \emph{chart morphism} \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2738	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\), and thus
2739	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q|_{T_I}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). The chart loci cover \(T\)
2740	  (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}), and on each overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two
2741	  presenting matrices are change-of-basis related by the invertible \(I\)-columns minor,
2742	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2743	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}); hence the composites \(T_I \xrightarrow{\varphi_I} U^I
2744	  \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with the chart immersions agree on overlaps after the
2745	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\). By the cocycle condition (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) these
2746	  chart-local morphisms glue to a single morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\)
2747	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}); this glued morphism is
2748	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x)\), the local-to-global inverse of the universal
2749	  property: pulling the tautological quotient back along it recovers \(\langle \mathcal{F},
2750	  q\rangle\) up to equivalence.
2751	\end{definition}
2752	
2753	The two blocks below carry the chart data of a quotient through a base change
2754	\(\psi : T' \to T\); they are the bridge underlying the two inverse laws of the
2755	representability theorem.
2756	
2757	\begin{lemma}[Chart composite of a pulled-back rank-\(d\) quotient]
2758	  \label{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback}
2759	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartComposite_rqPullback}
2760	  \uses{def:gr_chartComposite, def:gr_rankQuotient, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
2761	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) and a rank-\(d\) quotient \(y\) on \(T\), the chart
2762	  composite of the pulled-back quotient \(\psi^{*}y\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}) is the
2763	  \(\psi\)-pullback of the chart composite of \(y\), conjugated through the free-pullback
2764	  comparison:
2765	  \[
2766	    s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)
2767	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \circ \psi^{*}(s_I \circ y).
2768	  \]
2769	\end{lemma}
2770	\begin{proof}
2771	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
2772	  Unfolding the pullback action, \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)\) is the coordinate inclusion
2773	  \(s_I\) followed by \(Q_r^{-1}\) and \(\psi^{*}y\). The inverse free-pullback comparison
2774	  intertwines the index inclusion (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}): sliding
2775	  \(s_I\) across \(Q_r^{-1}\) turns it into \(Q_d^{-1}\) followed by \(\psi^{*}(s_I)\),
2776	  whence \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y) = Q_d^{-1} \circ \psi^{*}(s_I \circ y)\).
2777	\end{proof}
2778	
2779	\begin{lemma}[The chart locus of a pullback contains the preimage of the chart locus]
2780	  \label{lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback}
2781	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus_rqPullback}
2782	  \uses{def:chartLocus, lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2783	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
2784	  For \(\psi : T' \to T\) and a rank-\(d\) quotient \(y\) on \(T\), the \(\psi\)-preimage of
2785	  the \(I\)-chart locus of \(y\) is contained in the \(I\)-chart locus of the pulled-back
2786	  quotient:
2787	  \[
2788	    \psi^{-1}\bigl(\mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I)\bigr)
2789	      \;\le\; \mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I).
2790	  \]
2791	\end{lemma}
2792	\begin{proof}
2793	  \uses{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2794	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
2795	  Let \(U = \psi^{-1}(\mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I))\). The inclusion \(U \hookrightarrow T'\)
2796	  followed by \(\psi\) factors through the chart-locus inclusion
2797	  \(\iota : \mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I) \hookrightarrow T\), over which the chart composite
2798	  \(s_I \circ y\) pulls back to an isomorphism (\cref{def:chartLocus}). Hence its pullback
2799	  along the factoring composite is an isomorphism
2800	  (\cref{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}), and the pullback pseudofunctor comparison
2801	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) together with the chart-composite bridge
2802	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback}) identifies it with the pullback to \(U\) of the
2803	  chart composite of \(\psi^{*}y\). So \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)\) is invertible over \(U\),
2804	  i.e.\ \(U\) is one of the opens whose supremum defines
2805	  \(\mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I)\); taking the supremum gives
2806	  \(U \le \mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I)\).
2807	\end{proof}
2808	
2809	\begin{theorem}
2810	
2811	\leanok
2812	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
2813	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
2814	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
2815	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
2816	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
2817	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
2818	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2819	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
2820	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
2821	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2822	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
2823	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
2824	  %  contravariant functor
2825	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
2826	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
2827	  %  set of all equivalence classes
2828	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2829	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
2830	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
2831	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
2832	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
2833	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
2834	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
2835	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
2836	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
2837	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
2838	  \[
2839	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
2840	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2841	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
2842	    \qquad
2843	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
2844	      \;\longmapsto\;
2845	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
2846	  \]
2847	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
2848	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2849	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
2850	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
2851	  universal family.
2852	\end{theorem}
2853	
2854	\begin{proof}
2855	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
2856	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
2857	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
2858	    def:grassmannian_functor, lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi,
2859	    def:grPointOfRankQuotient, lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, def:chartLocus,
2860	    lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map, def:gr_tautologicalRankQuotient,
2861	    def:gr_chartMorphism, def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso,
2862	    lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback,
2863	    lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback, lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2864	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
2865	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
2866	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
2867	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
2868	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
2869	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
2870	
2871	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
2872	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
2873	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
2874	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
2875	  the composite
2876	  \[
2877	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
2878	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2879	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
2880	  \]
2881	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
2882	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
2883	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
2884	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
2885	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
2886	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
2887	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
2888	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
2889	
2890	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
2891	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
2892	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
2893	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
2894	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
2895	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
2896	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
2897	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
2898	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2899	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
2900	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2901	
2902	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
2903	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
2904	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
2905	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
2906	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2907	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
2908	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
2909	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) -- this is the overlap compatibility
2910	  \cref{lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat} -- so by the cocycle condition
2911	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
2912	  \(\varphi = \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle) :
2913	  T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient},
2914	  \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). This morphism is the candidate inverse; that it
2915	  is a two-sided inverse is the content of the two laws below.
2916	
2917	  \emph{Existence (the law \(\mathrm{right\_inv}\)):
2918	  \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle \sim \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\).}
2919	  The equivalence is checked over the chart cover \(\{T_I\}\). Over \(T_I\) the
2920	  morphism \(\varphi\) restricts to \(\varphi_I\) followed by the chart immersion
2921	  \(\iota_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chartMorphism}), and the restriction of the universal
2922	  quotient sheaf along \(\iota_I\) is identified with the free rank-\(d\) sheaf by
2923	  the chart restriction isomorphism
2924	  \(\phi_I : \iota_I^{*}\mathcal{U} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
2925	  (\cref{def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso}). Through this identification
2926	  \(\varphi^{*}u\) becomes \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I\), which by construction of the chart
2927	  morphism (\(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\)) equals \(q|_{T_I}\) in the
2928	  \(I\)-trivialisation (\cref{def:gr_chartMorphism}, \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2929	  The witnessing isomorphisms \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{F} \cong \mathcal{F}\) so
2930	  produced agree on overlaps -- the class is determined by \(\ker q\), which the
2931	  trivialisations match -- and assemble to a single isomorphism over \(T\), giving
2932	  \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle \sim \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\).
2933	
2934	  \emph{Uniqueness (the law \(\mathrm{left\_inv}\)):
2935	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle) = \psi\)
2936	  for every \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).} It suffices to check the equality
2937	  after restricting to each chart locus
2938	  \(\mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle, I)\) of the
2939	  pulled-back tautological quotient. Over such a locus the composite of its
2940	  inclusion with \(\psi\) factors through the \(\psi\)-preimage of the tautological
2941	  chart locus, which is contained in that locus by
2942	  \cref{lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback}; the chart-composite bridge
2943	  \cref{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback} identifies the chart data of
2944	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) with the \(\psi\)-pullback of the
2945	  tautological chart data, so the matrix presenting it there is the \(\psi\)-image
2946	  of the universal matrix \(X^I\). Hence the chart morphism classifying
2947	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) over this locus coincides with the
2948	  restriction of \(\psi\) into the chart \(U^I\) (\cref{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism}).
2949	  As the chart loci cover, the glued morphism
2950	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle)\) equals
2951	  \(\psi\) by the uniqueness clause of the glue-data universal property.
2952	
2953	  Existence and uniqueness exhibit \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}\) as a two-sided
2954	  inverse of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u\rangle\),
2955	  so the latter is bijective.
2956	
2957	  Therefore \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is a fine
2958	  moduli space: it represents \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\), so
2959	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2960	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).

2334	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
2335	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
2336	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
2337	
2338	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
2339	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
2340	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
2341	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
2342	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
2343	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
2344	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
2345	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
2346	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
2347	  map_id X := by
2348	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
2349	    ext z
2350	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
2351	    | _ x =>
2352	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
2353	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
2354	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
2355	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
2356	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
2357	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
2358	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
2359	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
2360	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
2361	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
2362	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
2363	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
2364	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
2365	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
2366	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
2367	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
2368	    ext z
2369	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
2370	    | _ x =>
2371	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
2372	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))

2538	/-- **Right-invertible matrices over a field have an invertible column minor**: a
2539	`d × r` matrix with a right inverse has an invertible `d × d` submatrix on the columns
2540	of some size-`d` subset `I`, enumerated by `I.orderIsoOfFin` (the spelling used by
2541	`chartComposite`). Pure linear algebra: the columns span `K^d`, a spanning set contains
2542	a basis, and a square matrix with independent columns is invertible. Project-local
2543	helper for the Nakayama covering step. -/
2544	private lemma exists_isUnit_submatrix {K : Type} [Field K] {d r : ℕ}
2545	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) K) (G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) K) (hMG : M * G = 1) :
2546	    ∃ (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d),
2547	      IsUnit (M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := by
2548	  classical
2549	  -- the columns of `M` span `K^d` (a right inverse makes `mulVec` surjective)
2550	  have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range M.col) = ⊤ := by
2551	    rw [← Matrix.range_mulVecLin, LinearMap.range_eq_top]
2552	    intro v
2553	    refine ⟨G.mulVec v, ?_⟩
2554	    show M.mulVec (G.mulVec v) = v
2555	    rw [Matrix.mulVec_mulVec, hMG, Matrix.one_mulVec]
2556	  -- extract a linearly independent spanning subset `b` of the column set
2557	  obtain ⟨b, hbsub, hbspan, hbind⟩ := exists_linearIndependent K (Set.range M.col)
2558	  rw [hspan] at hbspan
2559	  haveI : Fintype b := ((Set.finite_range M.col).subset hbsub).fintype
2560	  -- `b` is a basis, so it has exactly `d` elements
2561	  let B : Module.Basis b K (Fin d → K) :=
2562	    Module.Basis.mk hbind (by rw [Subtype.range_coe, hbspan])
2563	  have hcard : Fintype.card b = d := by
2564	    have h1 := Module.finrank_eq_card_basis B
2565	    rw [Module.finrank_pi K, Fintype.card_fin] at h1
2566	    exact h1.symm
2567	  -- choose a column index for each element of `b`
2568	  have hchoice : ∀ v : b, ∃ j : Fin r, M.col j = (v : Fin d → K) := fun v => hbsub v.2
2569	  choose φ hφ using hchoice
2570	  have hφinj : Function.Injective φ := fun u v huv => by
2571	    apply Subtype.ext
2572	    rw [← hφ u, ← hφ v, huv]
2573	  have hIcard : (Finset.univ.image φ).card = d := by
2574	    rw [Finset.card_image_of_injective _ hφinj, Finset.card_univ, hcard]
2575	  refine ⟨Finset.univ.image φ, hIcard, ?_⟩
2576	  -- each enumerated column lies in `b`
2577	  have hcol : ∀ j : Fin d,
2578	      M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)) ∈ b := by
2579	    intro j
2580	    obtain ⟨v, -, hv⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2581	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j).2
2582	    rw [← hv, hφ v]
2583	    exact v.2
2584	  set c : Fin d → b :=
2585	    fun j => ⟨M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)), hcol j⟩
2586	    with hcdef
2587	  have hcinj : Function.Injective c := by
2588	    intro j j' hjj
2589	    have hcols : M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r))
2590	        = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) :=
2591	      congrArg Subtype.val hjj
2592	    obtain ⟨v, -, hv⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2593	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j).2
2594	    obtain ⟨v', -, hv'⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2595	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j').2
2596	    have hvv' : v = v' := by
2597	      apply Subtype.ext
2598	      calc (v : Fin d → K)
2599	          = M.col (φ v) := (hφ v).symm
2600	        _ = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)) := by rw [hv]
2601	        _ = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) := hcols
2602	        _ = M.col (φ v') := by rw [hv']
2603	        _ = (v' : Fin d → K) := hφ v'
2604	    have hee : (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r))
2605	        = (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) := by
2606	      rw [← hv, ← hv', hvv']
2607	    exact ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard).injective (Subtype.ext hee)
2608	  -- the columns of the submatrix are the inclusion of `b` along the injective `c`

2611	        (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)))).col := by
2612	    have heq : (M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d =>
2613	        (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)))).col
2614	        = fun j => ((c j : Fin d → K)) := by
2615	      funext j
2616	      rfl

4027	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
4028	    (A.map ⇑f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map ⇑f := by

4034	/-- **Transport of the image matrix along a ring hom over the structure map**: pushing
4035	`M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` forward along any `incl : R^I_J →+* D` with
4036	`incl ∘ (R^I → R^I_J) = g` yields `(Y_J)⁻¹ · Y` for `Y := X^I` base-changed along `g`.
4037	Non-private CommRing form of the helper inside `GrassmannianCells.lean`. Project-local. -/
4038	lemma imageMatrix_map_ringHom (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
4039	    (hJ : J.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
4040	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) →+* D)
4041	    (g : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ →+* D)
4042	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
4043	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) = g) :
4044	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map ⇑incl
4045	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map ⇑g).submatrix id
4046	          (fun k : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
4047	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map ⇑g := by
4048	  have hcoe : ⇑incl ∘ ⇑(algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
4049	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) = ⇑g := by
4050	    rw [← RingHom.coe_comp, hincl]

4052	      = (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map ⇑incl *

4065	classifying-map form): for a ring map `f : R^I →+* K` under which the minor `P^I_J` is a
4066	unit, the transported point `lift(f) ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` presents the same `K`-point through
4067	chart `J` as `f` does through chart `I`. CommRing generalisation of
4068	`existence_chart_kpoint_eq` (whose `Field` hypothesis is unused); proof copied verbatim.
4069	Project-local. -/
4070	theorem chart_point_eq (d r : ℕ) {K : Type} [CommRing K] (I J : (theGlueData d r).J)

4072	    (hf : IsUnit (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2))) :

4075	          (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom)) ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι J
4076	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f) ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by

4078	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) hf with hf'def
4079	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (f'.comp (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom)
4080	        = CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom ≫
4081	          CommRingCat.ofHom f'
4082	      from by rw [← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp], Spec.map_comp, Category.assoc,

4112	/-- **Naturality of the chart morphism**: precomposing `φ_I : T_I ⟶ U^I` with any
4113	`p : W ⟶ T_I` is the morphism classified by the matrix presenting the quotient along
4114	`p ≫ ι_{T_I}` — `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` plus the entrywise transport

4116	lemma comp_chartMorphism {T W : Scheme.{0}} (d r : ℕ) (x : RankQuotient r d T)
4117	    (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (p : W ⟶ (chartLocus x I hI).toScheme)
4118	    [IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ (chartLocus x I hI).ι)).map
4119	      (chartComposite x I hI))] :
4120	    p ≫ chartMorphism d r x I hI
4121	      = W.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (MvPolynomial.aeval (R := ℤ)
4122	          (fun pq : Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I} =>
4123	            presentedMatrix x (p ≫ (chartLocus x I hI).ι) I hI pq.1 pq.2.1)).toRingHom) := by
4124	  have hmat : ∀ (a : Fin d) (b : Fin r),
4125	      presentedMatrix x (p ≫ (chartLocus x I hI).ι) I hI a b
4126	        = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)) (chartMatrix x I hI a b) := by

8:`glue` (equalizer-of-pushforwards construction), `glueLift`, and the full
170:/-- **Lift into the glued sheaf** (`def:gr_modules_glueHom`-adjacent primitive): a family of
1210:lemma pullback_map_jointly_faithful (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) {W W' : D.glued.Modules}
1236:    obtain ⟨i, y, rfl⟩ := D.ι_jointly_surjective pt
1778:  -- the double transpose of the full cast chain
2277:  -- REMAINING CORE (blueprint item (3)): the fully transposed component equation over
2409:the restricted product projections are jointly monic through the preservation
2740:  -- the double transpose of the full cast chain
===
noncomputable def glueLift (D : Scheme.GlueData)
    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))
    {W : D.glued.Modules}
    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
      k p.1 ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
        = k p.2 ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) :
    W ⟶ glue D M g _hC1 _hC2 :=
  equalizer.lift (Pi.lift k) (by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro p
    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.Pi.lift_π_assoc]
    exact hk p)

/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism

The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
morphisms. -/

/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/

Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.glued)) _
  haveI h₂ : Functor.IsContinuous
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (D.f i j).base ⋙ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i).opensFunctor)
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.U i))
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.U j)) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.V (i, j))) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _
      (NatIso.ofComponents
        (fun V => eqToIso (glueData_preimage_image_eq D i j V).symm)
        (fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by
      ext V x
      exact congr($(glueData_overlap_appIso_compat D i j (unop V)) x)) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

/-- **Joint faithfulness of the chart restrictions** (`lem:gr_modules_glue_unique`
core): two morphisms of sheaves of modules on the glued scheme that agree after
pullback to every chart agree. This is the separation half of the sheaf condition
over the chart cover `{ι_i}`: sections of the target sheaf are detected on the
chart-image opens `ι_i''(ι_i⁻¹ O)`, which cover any open `O` by the joint
surjectivity of the chart immersions. Engine for `tautologicalQuotient_epi`
(a morphism out of the glued sheaf is determined chart-locally). Project-local. -/
lemma pullback_map_jointly_faithful (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) {W W' : D.glued.Modules}
    {u v : W ⟶ W'}
    (h : ∀ i, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map u
        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map v) :
    u = v := by
  -- transfer the hypothesis to the site-level restriction functor, whose sections
  -- are concrete (`Γ(restrict W, V) = Γ(W, ι_i''V)`)
  have hres : ∀ i, (restrictFunctor (D.ι i)).map u = (restrictFunctor (D.ι i)).map v := by
    intro i
    calc (restrictFunctor (D.ι i)).map u
        = (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).hom.app W ≫
            (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map u ≫
            (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).inv.app W' :=
          (NatIso.naturality_2 (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)) u).symm
      _ = (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).hom.app W ≫
            (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map v ≫
            (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).inv.app W' := by rw [h i]
      _ = (restrictFunctor (D.ι i)).map v :=
          NatIso.naturality_2 (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)) v
  ext O x
  -- sheaf separation of the target over the cover `{ι_i''(ι_i⁻¹ O)}` of `O`
  refine TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq'
    (⟨W'.presheaf, W'.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab D.glued)
    (fun i => (D.ι i) ''ᵁ ((D.ι i) ⁻¹ᵁ O)) O
    (fun i => homOfLE ((D.ι i).image_preimage_le O)) ?_ _ _ ?_
  · intro pt hpt
    obtain ⟨i, y, rfl⟩ := D.ι_jointly_surjective pt
    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨i, y, hpt, rfl⟩
  · intro i
    -- naturality moves the restriction inside `u.app`/`v.app`; the chart-image
    -- agreement is `hres i` evaluated on sections (defeq through `restrict_obj`)
    have hu := congr($(u.mapPresheaf.naturality
      (homOfLE ((D.ι i).image_preimage_le O)).op) x)
    have hv := congr($(v.mapPresheaf.naturality
      (homOfLE ((D.ι i).image_preimage_le O)).op) x)
    have hres_app := congr($(congrArg
      (fun (m : (restrictFunctor (D.ι i)).obj W ⟶ (restrictFunctor (D.ι i)).obj W') =>
        Scheme.Modules.Hom.app m ((D.ι i) ⁻¹ᵁ O)) (hres i))
      ((W.presheaf.map (homOfLE ((D.ι i).image_preimage_le O)).op) x))
    exact hu.symm.trans (hres_app.trans hv)

/-- **Sections of the overlap base change**: on sections over `V ⊆ U_i`, the inverse
of `β_ij` is the restriction map of `N` along the opens identity
`ι_j⁻¹(ι_i''V) = (t_ij ≫ f_ji)''(f_ij⁻¹V)` (`glueData_preimage_image_eq`). All four
factors of `β_ij` are site-level pushforwards whose components act identically or as
a single presheaf restriction. Project-local. -/
lemma glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_inv_app_app (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i j : D.J)
The faithfulness proof ports directly to open covers of `T`. For existence (gluing morphisms), let me check what Mathlib offers for sheaf-hom gluing.

lemma chartQuotientMap_eq_matrixEndRect (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    chartQuotientMap d r I hI
      = matrixEndRect ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso
            (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))).inv)) := rfl

/-! ### Entry extraction and the matrix presentation of free-sheaf morphisms

(The block below was moved up from the chart-transport section so that the chart-loci
covering proof `chartLocus_isOpenCover` — which precedes that section — can use it.) -/

/-- The `p`-th **projection of the free sheaf** `O_X^d ⟶ O_X`, through the
biproduct/coproduct comparison. Companion of `SheafOfModules.ιFree`; project-local. -/
lemma pullback_conj_matrixEndRect {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V) {d r : ℕ}
    (N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(V, ⊤)) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).inv ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEndRect N) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
      = matrixEndRect (N.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p))) := by
  rw [matrixEndRect_pullback]
  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
/-- The free-pullback comparison intertwines index maps:
`p^*(freeMap g) ≫ Q_m = Q_n ≫ freeMap g`. Project-local — naturality of
`pullbackFreeIso` in the index. -/
noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
    (bundleTransitionData d r)
    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
    (fun p => (tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose d r p.1 p.2).mpr
      (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2))

/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property

The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.

Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
unchanged. -/

/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
noncomputable def tautologicalRankQuotient (d r : ℕ) : RankQuotient r d (scheme d r) where
  F := universalQuotient d r
  q := tautologicalQuotient d r
  epi := tautologicalQuotient_epi d r
  locFree := universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank d r

/-! ### The Nitsure §1 inverse construction: chart loci, chart matrices, chart morphisms

For a rank-`d` quotient `x = ⟨F, q⟩` on `T` and a size-`d` subset `I ⊆ Fin r`, the
*chart composite* is `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (the `I`-indexed coordinate inclusion
followed by the quotient map) and the *chart locus* `T_I ⊆ T` is the largest open on
which it restricts to an isomorphism. The loci are open by construction (a supremum of
opens), cover `T` (Nakayama at each point), and over `T_I` the quotient is presented by
a `d × r` matrix of sections whose `I`-minor is the identity — its complementary
entries determine a ring map `R^I ⟶ Γ(T_I, O)` and hence a morphism `T_I ⟶ U^I` by the
Γ–Spec adjunction. These glue to the inverse `grPointOfRankQuotient` of the universal
property. -/

/-- The **iso-locus** of a morphism of sheaves of modules on a scheme: the supremum
(union) of all opens `U` such that the pullback of `φ` to the open subscheme `U` is an
isomorphism. This is the largest open on which `φ` is invertible. Project-local
(Mathlib has no iso-locus for morphisms of sheaves of modules). -/
====

content:
namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
(`def:modules_annihilator`).

On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).

This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)

/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _

/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
  (annihilator F).subscheme

/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
`annihilator` is. -/
noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
  (annihilator F).subschemeι

/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)

content:
global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/

/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts

/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom

/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]

/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :

lemma chartMatrix_minor {T : Scheme.{0}} {r d : ℕ} (x : RankQuotient r d T)
    (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (p q : Fin d) :
    chartMatrix x I hI p (I.orderIsoOfFin hI q : Fin r) = if q = p then 1 else 0 := by
  have h1 : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (chartLocus x I hI).toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
        (I.orderIsoOfFin hI q : Fin r) ≫ chartMatrixHom x I hI ≫ projFree p
      = SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫ projFree p := by
    calc SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (chartLocus x I hI).toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
          (I.orderIsoOfFin hI q : Fin r) ≫ chartMatrixHom x I hI ≫ projFree p
        = (SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫
            SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) ≫
          chartMatrixHom x I hI ≫ projFree p :=
          congrArg (· ≫ chartMatrixHom x I hI ≫ projFree p)
            (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap
              (R := (chartLocus x I hI).toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
              (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) q).symm
      _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫
          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
            chartMatrixHom x I hI) ≫ projFree p := by
          simp only [Category.assoc]
      _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ projFree p :=
          congrArg (fun z => SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫ z ≫ projFree p)
            (freeMap_chartMatrixHom x I hI)
      _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫ projFree p :=
          congrArg (SheafOfModules.ιFree q ≫ ·) (Category.id_comp _)
  refine (congrArg unitEndSection h1).trans ?_
  rw [ιFree_projFree]
  by_cases h : q = p
  · rw [if_pos h, if_pos h]
    exact unitEndSection_id
  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg h]
    exact unitEndSection_zero

/-! ### Overlap compatibility of the chart morphisms (Nitsure §1 gluing step)

On the intersection `P = T_I ×_T T_J` of two chart loci both chart composites pull back
to isomorphisms, so the pulled-back quotient is presented by both matrices `M^I_P` and
`M^J_P`. The change-of-basis identity `M^I_P = (M^I_P)_J · M^J_P`
(`presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis`) identifies the `J`-classifying ring map with the
localization-transport `lift(aeval M^I_P) ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` of the `I`-classifying map, so the
two chart morphisms agree into the glued scheme by the glue condition (`chart_point_eq`,
the CommRing form of `existence_chart_kpoint_eq`). The block below supplies the
transports: invertibility along a composite (`isIso_pullback_map_comp`), the presented
matrix along a composite (`presentedMatrix_comp`) and along an equality of base
morphisms (`presentedMatrix_congr`), its own minor (`presentedMatrix_submatrix_self`),
the realisation `aeval(M^I)(X^I) = M^I` (`universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix`), the
image-matrix transport (`imageMatrix_map_ringHom`), and chart-morphism naturality
(`comp_chartMorphism`). -/

/-- Invertibility of a pulled-back morphism of modules persists along precomposition of
the base morphism: if `a^*c` is an isomorphism then so is `(p ≫ a)^*c`, via the
pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`. Project-local. -/
lemma isIso_pullback_map_comp {W V X : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V) (a : V ⟶ X)
    {M N : X.Modules} (c : M ⟶ N) [IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map c)] :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).map c) := by
  haveI h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map c)) :=
